name: Utah
tagline: The first state with a dedicated AI policy office, a fast-growing 'Silicon Slopes' data-center boom, and a centralized DTS that runs the nation's largest cloud cooperative — Utah is a digital-government bellwether.

execSummary.summary: Utah punches well above its size as a digital-government leader and is one of the most consequential SLG accounts in the Mountain West. Three things define the opportunity. First, Utah was the first state to create a dedicated Office of Artificial Intelligence Policy (SB 149, 2024) and an AI 'Learning Lab' regulatory sandbox; in late 2025 Governor Cox layered on a $10M 'Pro-Human' AI initiative and lawmakers funded $15M of compute capacity for state AI use — agencies are actively operationalizing governed AI. Second, Utah's IT is highly centralized: the Division of Technology Services (DTS), under CIO Alan Fuller, runs a ~$277M budget, 750+ projects, and the new MyUtah citizen portal, making DTS the dominant decision point. Third, the 'Silicon Slopes' data-center buildout (Creekstone's multi-gigawatt Delta 'gigasite', Box Elder, DataBank SL6, BluSky in Nephi) plus Texas Instruments' multi-fab investment make Utah a national AI-infrastructure flashpoint. For Dell, the buying motion runs through the Utah-led NASPO ValuePoint Cloud Solutions cooperative (Dell is an awarded supplier) and state cooperative contracts, with GovRAMP now the cloud-security gate.
opportunities: Governed AI for state agencies: $15M compute appropriation + $10M Pro-Human initiative open the door for private/on-prem AI on sensitive data under Utah's SB 149 framework.
AI-infrastructure demand tied to the Silicon Slopes data-center boom (PowerEdge, networking, services) and university research computing.
DTS modernization: legacy payroll/HR replacement, MyUtah portal, digital ID/verifiable credentials, and a whole-of-state cybersecurity strategy.
Endpoint, client, and lifecycle refresh across a centralized executive branch and the local-government devices DTS already protects.
Health AI: University of Utah's Utah Health AI Vault (UHAIV) build-out with state support.

stats.label: SB 149 / AI Policy Office
stats.note: First-in-nation AI policy office + Learning Lab

budget.intro: Utah budgets on an annual fiscal year (July 1–June 30) cycle. The 2026 Legislature approved a roughly $31.6B FY2027 budget, of which about $12.3B is state funds; Governor Cox's recommended plan was ~$30.7B and framed around 'responsible fiscal management.' There is no single 'state IT budget' line — most executive-branch technology spend is centralized in DTS (~$277M), with named one-time AI and compute items funded in the 2026 session.

stats.label: State funds
stats.note: Portion of FY2027 from state sources

notes: Utah uses an annual fiscal year — figures are single-year unless noted; do not compare directly to biennial-budget states.
DTS centralizes most executive-branch IT, so the practical 'tech budget' concentrates there rather than spread across agencies.
AI and compute line items are one-time appropriations from the 2026 session — confirm recurring vs. one-time before forecasting.

initiatives.name: Office of AI Policy + 'Pro-Human' AI Initiative
initiatives.summary: SB 149 (2024) made Utah the first state with a dedicated Office of Artificial Intelligence Policy and an AI 'Learning Lab' regulatory sandbox (sunset extended to July 2027 by 2025 amendments). In Dec 2025 Gov. Cox announced a $10M 'Pro-Human' AI initiative and a Pro-Human AI Task Force (members named Feb 2026), with workstreams including state-government AI deployment. Agencies need governed, auditable AI — an opening for private/on-prem AI on sensitive workloads.

initiatives.name: Silicon Slopes Data-Center Buildout
initiatives.summary: Utah has become a national AI-infrastructure flashpoint: Creekstone's Delta 'gigasite' (planned 10GW+), Box Elder and Nephi (BluSky) projects, DataBank's SL6 expansion, and Texas Instruments' multi-fab semiconductor investment. Gov. Cox has publicly defended the buildout on national-security grounds. This is the strongest tailwind for Dell AI-infrastructure and ISG demand in the state.

initiatives.name: DTS Modernization & MyUtah
initiatives.summary: DTS (~$277M, 750+ projects) launched the MyUtah citizen portal (public Feb 2026), is replacing legacy payroll/HR systems, piloting digital verifiable credentials/digital ID, and standing up an internal AI program to 'give state employees superpowers.' Centralized IT means a single, high-leverage modernization buyer.

initiatives.name: Whole-of-State Cybersecurity + GovRAMP
initiatives.summary: Utah joined GovRAMP as a participating government (Mar 2025), making GovRAMP authorization the practical cloud-security gate for state data. DTS runs a whole-of-state strategy — endpoint protection deployed to 30,000+ local-government devices plus the executive branch, and 30,000+ people trained. Any Dell cloud/as-a-service offering touching state data should map to GovRAMP early.

initiatives.name: University Research & Health AI (UHAIV)
initiatives.summary: The University of Utah is building the Utah Health AI Vault (UHAIV) with state support, and USHE institutions are significant independent research-computing buyers. Higher ed and academic-medical AI are strong, governed-data fits for Dell AI infrastructure and secure compute.

procurement.intro: Utah's IT is unusually centralized. The Division of Technology Services (DTS), within the Department of Government Operations, is the executive-branch IT provider and a primary decision point. Statewide buying runs through the Division of Purchasing and General Services (state cooperative contracts) and, notably, the Utah-led NASPO ValuePoint Cloud Solutions cooperative — Utah is the lead state for the national cloud cooperative used by SLG entities nationwide.

vehicles.name: NASPO ValuePoint Cloud Solutions
vehicles.detail: Utah-led national cooperative; Dell Marketing LP is an awarded supplier. NASPO is moving to a more flexible 10-year cloud/software contract that lets vendors join mid-term. Primary cooperative vehicle.

vehicles.name: Utah State Cooperative Contracts
vehicles.detail: Division of Purchasing and General Services pre-competed contracts open to state agencies and local government. Confirm Dell agreements and resellers are current.

vehicles.name: GovRAMP authorization
vehicles.detail: Not a contract, but the practical cloud-security gate for any cloud/as-a-service offering touching state data (Utah joined as a participating government Mar 2025).

entities.name: Division of Technology Services (DTS)
entities.role: Centralized executive-branch IT, modernization, and cyber — the dominant statewide tech decision point.

entities.name: Division of Purchasing & General Services
entities.role: State procurement authority; runs cooperative contracts and the NASPO lead-state cloud solicitation.

entities.name: Office of AI Policy (Dept. of Commerce)
entities.role: First-in-nation AI policy office and Learning Lab; shapes how agencies adopt governed AI.

procurement.competition: Utah is a centralized, cooperative-driven market where DTS concentrates executive-branch decisions and the NASPO cooperative shapes cloud buys. Differentiation comes from GovRAMP-ready offerings, governed/on-prem AI capability for sensitive data, and services that map to the data-center buildout and DTS modernization rather than price alone — Dell already holds NASPO ValuePoint cloud awards to build on.

powerStructure.name: Spencer Cox
powerStructure.title: Governor
powerStructure.note: Champions Utah's 'Pro-Human' AI agenda and the data-center buildout; vocal on AI policy and state authority.

powerStructure.name: Alan Fuller
powerStructure.title: State CIO & DTS Director
powerStructure.note: Leads centralized IT since 2021; drives AI-for-employees, customer experience, digital ID, and whole-of-state cyber.

powerStructure.name: Office of AI Policy
powerStructure.title: AI policy & Learning Lab
powerStructure.note: First-in-nation AI policy office; runs the regulatory sandbox shaping agency AI adoption.

powerStructure.name: Division of Purchasing & General Services
powerStructure.title: State procurement
powerStructure.note: Owns cooperative contracts and the NASPO lead-state cloud cooperative — key for vehicle strategy.

news.title: MyUtah citizen portal launches publicly; Pro-Human AI Task Force members named
news.impact: DTS digital-government modernization goes live; AI governance moves from policy to execution across state services.

news.title: Legislature approves ~$31.6B FY2027 budget incl. $15M AI compute capacity
news.impact: Dedicated compute funding signals real state AI workloads — an opening for governed AI infrastructure.

news.title: Gov. Cox unveils $10M 'Pro-Human' AI initiative at Utah's first AI Summit
news.impact: State backs AI workforce, curricula, and government deployment — agencies will need governed AI approaches.

news.title: Silicon Slopes data-center buildout accelerates (Creekstone Delta gigasite, Nephi, Box Elder)
news.impact: Utah becomes a national AI-infrastructure hub — strong tailwind for Dell ISG/AI-infrastructure demand.

news.title: SB 149 amendments narrow AI disclosure rules; sandbox sunset extended to July 2027
news.impact: Utah refines its first-in-nation AI law, keeping the Learning Lab open — continued governed-AI demand.

news.title: Utah joins GovRAMP as a participating government
news.impact: GovRAMP becomes the practical cloud-security gate for state data; clear it early on as-a-service deals.

dellActions: Lead with governed AI: tie Dell on-prem/private AI and secure compute to Utah's SB 149 framework, the $10M Pro-Human initiative, and the $15M state AI compute appropriation for sensitive-data workloads.
Ride the Silicon Slopes data-center boom — position Dell ISG / AI-infrastructure with operators and the university research-computing and health-AI (UHAIV) buyers.
Make DTS the anchor account: align to MyUtah, legacy payroll/HR modernization, digital ID, and the AI-for-employees program in a centralized, single-decision-point IT org.
Confirm GovRAMP readiness for any cloud/as-a-service offering touching state data before pursuing deals — it is now Utah's cloud-security gate.
Leverage the Dell NASPO ValuePoint Cloud Solutions award (Utah-led cooperative) and validate state cooperative-contract and reseller coverage for the agencies you're targeting.
